summary: Tested Admin Protection + Production Safety Lockdown feature (X-Admin-Key gate on /api/admin/*, frontend AdminAccessGate, footer link removal, honeypot on lead forms). All 32 backend pytest tests pass (existing 21 + new TestAdminLeads/TestHoneypot cases). Frontend gate flow, wrong/correct key handling, footer, and honeypot invisibility verified via Playwright.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: verify_admin_access correctly returns 401 for missing key and 403 for wrong/disabled key as required; honeypot short-circuit in create_lead returns a synthetic LeadSubmission without persisting - correct behavior.

AdminLeads.jsx: gate correctly blocks rendering of lead table until a valid key is verified via a live API call (not just a local check), and clears session key + re-shows gate on 401/403 from any subsequent call - good defense in depth.
adminAuth.js: uses sessionStorage (not localStorage) with soft TTL, correctly implemented.
Footer.jsx: no admin link/text present, verified on homepage and /pricing.

HoneypotField.jsx: field is style-hidden via opacity:0 + off-screen absolute position + tabIndex=-1 + aria-hidden - solid honeypot implementation, confirmed truly invisible to users via computed style/bounding box check.
No code changes needed based on this test pass.
seed_data_creation: Test leads created with TEST_ prefix and test_admin_/test_hp_/legacy_ email prefixes via pytest run (not cleaned up, but consistent with existing baseline test data pattern already in DB from iteration_1)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Admin protection (401/403/200), gate UI (correct/wrong key), footer link removal, and honeypot (server-side no-op with 201 fake success) are all verified working end-to-end. No regressions to the original 21 lead-capture tests. If future work touches this area, re-run /app/backend/tests/test_leads.py plus a quick UI check of /admin/leads gate flow.
rca of the issue: N/A - no bugs found in this pass
